relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Aaron, his sons, and all Israel receive the command.
Slaughter is no longer detached from holy accountability.
Blood/life shed apart from the tent carries guilt.
Field sacrifice is redirected to YHWH's presence.
Blood/life and fat are brought into peace/well-being offering before YHWH.
Israel is turned away from wild goat powers and rival rites.
Native and sojourner share the offering boundary.
Offerings are brought to YHWH at the tent entrance.
The soul/life of flesh is in the blood/life, given for covering on the altar.
No native or sojourner eats blood/life.
Hunted blood/life is poured out and covered with dust.
All flesh carries soul/life in blood/life.
Carcass eating brings washing and waiting.
Refusing the washing leaves iniquity carried.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 17 teaches reverence for life: blood is not casual consumption, sacrifice is not private magic, and the life of flesh returns to YHWH in holy order.
